notes: Concentrated fabric softener exists (All, Tide) and eco-refill brands like Blueland already own this space

product_name: Duru Olive Oil Laundry Soap Bar
notes: Heritage Park and other artisan brands already make olive oil delicates soap, market is small
Clorox Bleach Pen existed (discontinued but served this need), gel bleach is niche

product_name: Bingo Matic Washing Machine Cleaner Powder
notes: Affresh and Tide Washing Machine Cleaner dominate this category
Thousands of cedar sachet brands on Amazon and Etsy, very crowded
Too niche - how many people have Turkish towels? Tiny addressable market
Woolite already owns wool/cashmere care in US, lanolin formulas exist

product_name: Duru Anti-Static Dryer Sheets with Eucalyptus
notes: Natural dryer sheets exist (Seventh Generation, Grab Green), eucalyptus not compelling
Tide, Persil, All have color-protect pods already in US market
Arm & Hammer Washing Soda, Borax, OxiClean exist - very crowded booster category
Scent beads are commoditized, Downy owns category, competing on scent alone is weak

product_name: Vanish Oxi Action Crystal White Powder
notes: OxiClean dominates oxygen bleach category in US

product_name: Ariel Matic Foam Control Low Suds Formula
notes: All HE detergents sold in US are low-suds formula, this is standard not unique

product_name: Fairy Dish Soap for Delicate Hand Washing
notes: Using dish soap for laundry is cultural hack, not compelling as branded product
Shout Color Catcher sheets already solve color run problem in US

product_name: Rosewood Cedar Blocks with Essential Oils
notes: Cedar blocks are commodity product, hundreds of brands on Amazon
Mesh laundry bags are commodity, sold by dozens of brands for $10-15
Arm & Hammer carpet powder, Resolve powder exist - mature category
